content:
Calling All Legal Graduates – Ready to Start Your Legal Career?
One of the most common conversations we have with recent graduates is how difficult it can feel to secure that first legal role when every opportunity seems to require experience.
The reality is that many firms are looking for candidates who can demonstrate the right attitude, strong communication skills, commercial awareness, and a genuine passion for law. Experience is valuable, but it isn't everything.
If you're a recent Law graduate, LPC graduate, SQE candidate, or aspiring legal professional looking to secure your first position as a Legal Assistant, Paralegal, Legal Administrator, or Trainee, don't be discouraged by setbacks. The right opportunity is out there.
A few tips:
 ✅ Tailor your CV to each application
 ✅ Highlight transferable skills from part-time work, placements, and volunteering
 ✅ Network with legal professionals and recruiters
 ✅ Stay informed on legal developments and the firms you're applying to
 ✅ Be persistent – many successful legal careers start with a single opportunity
We're currently working with a number of firms that are open to considering ambitious junior candidates, so if you're looking for your first step into the legal sector, feel free to reach out for a confidential chat.
What was your first role in law, and what advice would you give to graduates starting their journey?
#LegalCareers #LawGraduates #ParalegalJobs #TraineeSolicitor #LegalRecruitment #SQE #LPC #GraduateJobs #LegalJobs #CareerAdvice
comment: Early applications often hinge less on CV tailoring and more on visible work product like drafting samples; 'commercial awareness' tends to be too vague to differentiate juniors.
